'''''Episode I: Qui-Gon Jinn''''' is a one-shot comic book retelling events from [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] from the perspective of [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]]. The issue was written by [[Ryder Windham]], illustrated by [[Robert Teranishi]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[July 7]], [[1999]]. Originally scheduled for a [[June 2]] release,<ref name="Collector">{{CollectorCite|6|Around the Galaxy}}</ref> it was later swapped with ''[[Episode I: Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'', which was released on June 2 instead.

==Publisher's summary==
''Though he has been branded a maverick, a troublesome Jedi with a propensity to follow his own aptitude for the Force in all living things, Qui-Gon Jinn is undoubtedly a Jedi Knight of the first order. But his greatest critic is his own student, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and Qui-Gon knows that now is the time for him to prove the strength of his own convictions, for nothing less than the fate of the galaxy rests upon his shoulders!''<ref name="Dark Horse" />
